edges.description: Transformed squamous cells invade the hypopharyngeal wall and can spread to regional lymph nodes or distant sites.
edges.source: Malignant Transformation of Postcricoid Squamous Epithelium

edges.description: Local mass effect and impaired hypopharyngeal transit produce swallowing difficulty.
Local extension can involve laryngeal function, vocal fold motion, and airway patency.
Progressing squamous tumors can acquire immune-evasion programs that are relevant to checkpoint inhibitor treatment selection.
Persistent checkpoint engagement suppresses cytotoxic T-cell function and supports tumor immune escape.
edges.target: T Cell Exhaustion and Immune Escape

edges.source: Radical Radiotherapy for Early-Stage Hypopharyngeal SCC
edges.target: Malignant Transformation of Postcricoid Squamous Epithelium

edges.source: Surgery-Based Therapy for Resectable Hypopharyngeal Carcinoma
Induction Chemoimmunotherapy for Locally Advanced Hypopharyngeal SCC
Induction Chemoimmunotherapy for Locally Advanced Hypopharyngeal SCC

nodes.description: Postcricoid and hypopharyngeal squamous tumors can be treated with PD-1 pathway blockade in chemoimmunotherapy protocols, reflecting checkpoint- mediated suppression of antitumor T-cell activity rather than direct targeting of invasion alone.

biological_processes: negative regulation of T cell mediated immunity

evidence.reference_title: PD-1/PD-L1 axis in cancer: Regulatory mechanisms and therapeutic targets.
evidence.snippet: However, cancer cells exploit the PD-1/PD-L1 axis to cause immune escape in cancer development and progression.

evidence.reference_title: Personalized Treatment Strategies via Integration of Gene Expression Biomarkers in Molecular Profiling of Laryngeal Cancer
evidence.snippet: The review focuses on indicators, including PD-L1, CTLA-4, and tumor mutational burden (TMB) in predicting immunotherapy responses, highlighting recent developments in our understanding of the intricate interactions between tumor genetics and the immune milieu.

nodes.description: Difficulty swallowing is a common presenting symptom because the tumor occupies the postcricoid hypopharynx near the pharyngoesophageal junction.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Conundrum: Unveiling a Unique Presentation of Hypopharyngeal Carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: A 33-year-old female presented at a tertiary care hospital in Western India with hoarseness of voice, difficulty in swallowing, productive cough, and neck pain for two months with an abrupt increase in the severity of all symptoms in two days.

nodes.description: Phase II data support induction camrelizumab plus modified TPF as an emerging organ-preservation strategy for locally advanced hypopharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma, with surgery and adjuvant treatment reserved for non-responders in the studied protocol.
nodes.id: Induction Chemoimmunotherapy for Locally Advanced Hypopharyngeal SCC

evidence.reference_title: Camrelizumab-based induction chemoimmunotherapy in locally advanced stage hypopharyngeal carcinoma: phase II clinical trial.
evidence.snippet: This phase II trial aimed to determine the efficacy and safety of induction chemoimmunotherapy of camrelizumab plus modified TPF in locally advanced hypopharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma (LA HSCC) (NCT04156698).

evidence.reference_title: Camrelizumab-based induction chemoimmunotherapy in locally advanced stage hypopharyngeal carcinoma: phase II clinical trial.
evidence.snippet: After induction therapy, the ORR was 82.4% (42/51), meeting the prespecified endpoint.

nodes.description: Tumor extension through the hypopharyngeal wall and spread to nodal or distant sites drives staging, prognosis, and multimodality treatment selection.

evidence.reference_title: Imaging Recommendations for Diagnosis, Staging and Management of Larynx and Hypopharynx Cancer
evidence.snippet: Imaging also plays a major role in its staging, including local disease extent, nodal and distant metastatic status, as well as to assess response to therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Personalized Treatment Strategies via Integration of Gene Expression Biomarkers in Molecular Profiling of Laryngeal Cancer
evidence.snippet: The review focuses on indicators, including PD-L1, CTLA-4, and tumor mutational burden (TMB) in predicting immunotherapy responses, highlighting recent developments in our understanding of the intricate interactions between tumor genetics and the immune milieu.

nodes.description: Squamous epithelial cells in the postcricoid hypopharynx undergo malignant transformation, with increased proliferation and acquisition of invasive behavior.
nodes.id: Malignant Transformation of Postcricoid Squamous Epithelium

evidence.reference_title: Imaging Recommendations for Diagnosis, Staging and Management of Larynx and Hypopharynx Cancer
evidence.snippet: Squamous cell carcinoma is the most common histological type.

nodes.description: Organ-preserving radiotherapy is an evidence-supported option for selected early-stage hypopharyngeal SCC, including small postcricoid-region subsets.
nodes.id: Radical Radiotherapy for Early-Stage Hypopharyngeal SCC

evidence.reference_title: Early-stage hypopharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma treated with radical radiotherapy at a uniform dose of 70 Gy in 35 fractions: a single-center study.
evidence.snippet: This study supports radical radiotherapy as an effective approach for optimal tumor control in patients with early stage HSCC.

nodes.description: Surgery-based therapy, often followed by adjuvant radiotherapy with or without chemotherapy depending on stage and risk features, is used for resectable hypopharyngeal/postcricoid disease.
nodes.id: Surgery-Based Therapy for Resectable Hypopharyngeal Carcinoma

evidence.reference_title: Survival analyses of different treatment modalities and clinical stage for hypopharyngeal carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: SBT can obtain significant survival benefits when compared with definitive CRT for the whole cohort of patients.

evidence.reference_title: Imaging Recommendations for Diagnosis, Staging and Management of Larynx and Hypopharynx Cancer
evidence.snippet: Advanced disease is treated with multimodality of either chemoradiotherapy or surgery followed by adjuvant radiotherapy with or without concurrent chemotherapy.

nodes.description: Suppressed tumor-infiltrating T cells can lose effective cytokine production and cytolytic activity, allowing immune escape within the tumor microenvironment.
nodes.id: T Cell Exhaustion and Immune Escape

evidence.reference_title: T-cell exhaustion in the tumor microenvironment.
evidence.snippet: The exhausted T cells in the tumor microenvironment show overexpressed inhibitory receptors, decreased effector cytokine production and cytolytic activity, leading to the failure of cancer elimination.
